content_html:
Self-hosted email security, mail server, and Nextcloud — one stack, one Docker Compose file.
Open-source spam & malware filtering, S/MIME & PGP encryption, DKIM/DMARC/ARC, mailboxes, webmail, and SSO.
Pro adds time-of-click link protection — the inbound-link defense you'd otherwise pay Proofpoint or Mimecast for.

Features · Pro · Pricing · Docs · Support

Deployable as a gateway in front of Microsoft 365 / Google Workspace / Exchange, as a full mail server with built-in mailboxes, or in hybrid mode.

About

Hermes Secure Email Gateway is a Free Open Source Secure Email Gateway and Email Server.

It provides spam, virus, and malware protection through Apache SpamAssassin, ClamAV, and Amavisd-new; full in-transit and at-rest email encryption via SMTP TLS, S/MIME, PGP, encrypted PDF (powered by CipherMail), and Dovecot mail-crypt; email archiving; integrated mailbox hosting on Dovecot with per-user quotas, aliases, shared folders, Sieve rules, vacation auto-reply, and mobile-device autoconfiguration; file sync, webmail, calendars (CalDAV), and contacts (CardDAV) through Nextcloud; a local user directory and single sign-on via OpenLDAP and Authelia — with multi-factor authentication via TOTP, WebAuthn, and Duo Push; and modern email authentication standards including SPF, DKIM signing and verification, DMARC, and ARC through OpenDKIM, OpenDMARC, and OpenARC.

Hermes combines these Open Source technologies under one unified web-based administration console for easy management of your organization's inbound and outbound email, mailbox users, encryption keys, and authentication policies. End users get a self-service portal for managing their own signatures, sieve rules, vacation messages, app passwords, and mobile-device profiles.

Hermes can be deployed three ways:

  • As a gateway in front of an existing mail solution (in-house Exchange / Postfix, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365). Hermes scans, filters, encrypts, and relays mail to your backend.
  • As a full mail server with built-in mailbox hosting, webmail, file sync, calendars, and contacts. No external mail backend required.
  • As a hybrid — gateway for some domains AND mail server for others on the same install. Relay (gateway) domains and mailbox (mail server) domains coexist in a single Hermes deployment.

This Docker Edition packages the entire stack as a set of containers managed by Docker Compose, replacing the legacy bare-metal Ubuntu installer with a portable, reproducible deployment.

Looking for a managed version? Hermes is self-hosted by design. If you want to support development and get vendor support, see Pro pricing — per-server licensing, no per-mailbox fees.

Editions

Hermes ships in two editions:

Edition License What you get
Community AGPL v3 — free, open source The entire mail gateway and email server stack. All core security, encryption, mailbox hosting, and administration features.
Pro Commercial — see EULA Everything in Community plus 6 advanced features (see Pro Features below). Pricing →

A Pro license is purchased separately. Community Edition needs no license file and works fully without one.

The project is licensed under AGPLv3 (LICENSE); a small set of proprietary Pro Edition files are excluded from the AGPL grant and governed by the Pro EULA. See LICENSING.md for the authoritative breakdown — the per-file header controls which license applies.

Features

A condensed list. See hermesseg.io/features for the full feature page with screenshots.

Mail security (Community)

  • Spam protection (Apache SpamAssassin, postscreen, RBL configuration, sender/recipient/network block-allow lists, global sender filters)
  • Anti-virus protection (ClamAV via Amavisd-new, with built-in feeds: ClamAV official + URLhaus)
  • Malware feeds management (managed via Fangfrisch) — configure additional 3rd-party signature feeds including SaneSecurity, MalwarePatrol, SecuriteInfo, TwinWave, ClamPunch, RFXN, InterServer, Ditekshen, and more
  • Per-recipient spam/virus/file policies
  • Custom message rules, score overrides, custom file expressions/extensions/rules
  • Quarantine, message-history search, queue management, train as spam/ham, release to recipient, download messages

Encryption and authentication (Community)

  • In-transit email encryption: SMTP TLS, S/MIME, PGP (via CipherMail)
  • At-rest encryption: Dovecot mail crypt
  • Encrypted PDF email for recipients without S/MIME or PGP
  • Multi-factor authentication (Authelia): TOTP, WebAuthn, Duo Push
  • Local LDAP user store (built-in OpenLDAP) for admins, mailbox users, and relay users
  • App passwords for SMTP / IMAP / DAV clients (separate from main account password)
  • Free Let's Encrypt (ACME) TLS certificates — automated issuance and auto-renewal for the admin console and per mailbox domain (SAN), plus import/CSR support for 3rd-party certificates
  • haveibeenpwned password check integration

Email standards (Community)

  • SPF check, DKIM check + sign, DMARC verification (OpenDMARC)
  • ARC (Authenticated Received Chain) signing and verification (OpenARC) — preserves authentication results across trusted forwarders
  • DKIM key generation and management (UI)
  • Multi-instance OpenDKIM for differential outbound-sign vs inbound-verify behavior
  • DMARC report aggregation and reporting

Email server / mailbox hosting (Community)

  • Local mailbox hosting (Dovecot 2.4) with IMAPS / POP3S / Submission (587/465) / LMTP
  • Per-domain and per-mailbox quotas
  • Mailbox aliases and forwarders
  • Shared mailboxes and shared folders
  • User-defined Sieve rules
  • Vacation auto-reply with date scoping and per-address filtering
  • Mobile device autoconfiguration via signed .mobileconfig profiles (iOS) and CalDAV/CardDAV autodiscovery
  • Personal email signatures (rich HTML, with template gallery)
  • External Sender Banner (inbound mail from outside the org gets a visual banner)

Nextcloud integration (Community)

  • File sync (Nextcloud Files)
  • Webmail (Nextcloud Mail)
  • Calendars (CalDAV) and contacts (CardDAV)
  • Single sign-on via Authelia OIDC
  • Pre-provisioning of Nextcloud user accounts on first login

Admin and user experience (Community)

  • Modern AdminLTE 4 / Bootstrap 5 administrator console
  • User self-service portal (per-mailbox)
  • Real-time dashboard with system-resource monitoring
  • Message statistics with visual charts
  • Scheduled tasks UI (DB-backed Ofelia job management)
  • Searchable system event logs
  • Internal CA management (S/MIME)
  • DNS resolver (Unbound, local-recursive)
  • Console host and domain management

Pro features

Pro Edition adds the following capabilities on top of everything in Community. Full Pro feature page →

Pro Feature What it does
Email disclaimers Per-domain outbound disclaimer templates, applied at the milter level. Form-based template renderer with reusable templates.
Organizational signatures Centrally-managed per-domain employee signature templates with placeholder substitution (employee name, title, phone, email, department, organization info). Renders on every outbound message. Community Edition has Personal Signatures (per-user, free-form) only.
Intrusion Prevention (IPS) Web UI for managing Fail2ban jails, ban thresholds, ban duration, whitelists. Real-time view of active bans.
Console firewall Web UI for managing the host firewall protecting the admin console (port allowlisting, source-IP restriction).
LDAP RemoteAuth Per-domain pass-through authentication to one or more external LDAP servers (including Microsoft Active Directory). End users authenticate against your existing directory; Hermes provisions mailboxes on first successful login. Supports STARTTLS and LDAPS.
Link Guard Time-of-click URL protection for inbound mail — comparable to Microsoft Safe Links and Proofpoint URL Defense. Links are rewritten to a Hermes redirect and the destination's reputation is checked at the moment the user clicks — catching links weaponized after delivery. Layered verdicts (heuristics + URLhaus / OpenPhish blocklist feeds + optional Google Safe Browsing / VirusTotal), open-redirect detection, an operator-managed list of abused cloud-storage / redirector hosts, and optional guarded redirect-chain following. Admin-configurable per-tier actions and outbound link restoration. Runs in-stack or on a separate host.

Architecture

Hermes SEG Docker Edition runs as 19 containers orchestrated by Docker Compose:

Container Purpose
hermes_unbound Recursive DNS resolver for the stack
hermes_db_server MariaDB — Hermes, Authelia, Nextcloud, OpenDMARC, CipherMail, Syslog databases
hermes_ofelia Scheduled task runner (cron replacement)
hermes_nginx Reverse proxy + SSL termination (admin console, user portal, Nextcloud, CipherMail UI)
hermes_authelia SSO portal with MFA (TOTP / WebAuthn / Duo Push)
hermes_authelia_redis Session store for Authelia
hermes_commandbox CFML application server (Lucee) — hosts admin console + user portal
hermes_postfix_dkim Postfix MTA + OpenDKIM signer / verifier
hermes_dmarc OpenDMARC verifier + report aggregator
hermes_openarc OpenARC chain signer / verifier
hermes_mail_filter Amavisd-new + SpamAssassin + ClamAV content filter
hermes_body_milter Body-modification milter — signatures, disclaimers, external-sender banners, and Link Guard link rewriting / restore
hermes_linkguard Link Guard time-of-click safe-links endpoint (URL rewriting + click-time reputation)
hermes_ciphermail S/MIME, PGP, encrypted-PDF encryption gateway
hermes_fail2ban Brute-force prevention (Dovecot, Authelia jails)
hermes_dovecot IMAP / POP3 / Submission / LMTP / Sieve server
hermes_ldap OpenLDAP — local user directory (admins, mailboxes, relay users)
hermes_nextcloud File sync, webmail, CalDAV, CardDAV
hermes_nextcloud_redis Cache + locking backend for Nextcloud

Storage topology

Hermes splits storage across five independent tiers so each can live on the right type of disk for its workload:

Tier Default path Contents Storage profile
Config install root (implicit) Repo working tree, generated config, secrets, .env Fast SSD; sized by repo location
Data /mnt/data Databases, service logs, mail-filter state, Postfix queue Fast SSD; sized for DB growth and log retention. High write rate, backup-critical.
Archive /mnt/archive Amavis quarantine archive Cheap bulk; sized for retention policy × quarantine inflow. Grows unboundedly, cold access.
Vmail /mnt/vmail Dovecot mailboxes Cheap bulk; sized for users × quota
Nextcloud /mnt/files Nextcloud app + user files + Redis cache Cheap bulk; sized for user file storage

Smaller deployments can collapse tiers — point Archive, Vmail, and Nextcloud at the same path as Data for a single-disk install. The installer prompts for each path; all four operator-selected mount points are mandatory (empty values risk relative path resolution during compose substitution).

See the canonical reference at docs.deeztek.com · Storage Topology (5 tiers) or docs/install/storage-topology.md in the repo.

Requirements

Item Recommendation
Host OS Any Linux distribution capable of running Docker Engine 24.0+ and Compose v2. Tested reference: Ubuntu 24.04 Server.
Docker Engine 24.0+
Docker Compose v2
CPU 4 vCPUs minimum, more for higher mail volume
RAM 8 GB minimum, 16 GB+ recommended for production
Disk Config / OS disk: 120 GB minimum — OS, Docker engine, the full Hermes image set + running containers, the repo, and install/service logs. The four data tiers (Data, Archive, Vmail, Nextcloud) are sized to your anticipated usage — see Storage topology. For a small single-disk test install where all tiers collapse onto one disk, ~275 GB total (thin-provisioned) is a comfortable starting point.
Network Static IP or DHCP reservation. See docs for the full inbound + outbound port list (anti-spam services need TCP/2703, UDP/6277, TCP/24441, etc.)

Optional but recommended

For a production install, give each storage tier its own physical or virtual disk — separate from the OS disk and from each other. The tiers have deliberately different I/O and growth profiles, so isolating them lets you match disk to workload and size/expand each independently:

Tier Disk it wants Why a dedicated disk
Data Fast SSD High write rate (databases, logs, Postfix queue) and backup-critical — isolating it from the OS disk is the single biggest performance win
Archive Commodity bulk Quarantine archive grows unboundedly with retention; keep that growth off the DB/OS disk
Vmail Commodity bulk Dovecot mailboxes scale with users × quota; size and grow independently
Nextcloud Commodity bulk User files + Redis cache; size for file-storage growth independently

None of this is strictly required — small or test deployments can collapse the tiers onto one disk (point Archive, Vmail, and Nextcloud at the same path as Data; see Storage topology above). But for any install carrying real mail volume, a dedicated disk per tier is the recommended layout: it keeps the latency-sensitive Data tier fast, and prevents unbounded quarantine / mailbox / file growth from ever filling the database disk.

If you don't want a secondary drive for a given tier, simply create the directory on your primary disk (e.g., mkdir /mnt/data /mnt/archive /mnt/vmail /mnt/files) and point the installer at it.

Installation

1. Clone the repository

Clone the repository wherever you'd like Hermes installed:

sudo git clone https://github.com/deeztek/Hermes-Secure-Email-Gateway.git
cd Hermes-Secure-Email-Gateway

2. Run the installer

sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh

The installer runs the full install in a single session and takes 10–30 minutes on a fresh host (mostly image downloads and fail2ban container build).

The installer will:

  1. Display the Pro EULA and ask for acceptance (Community Edition users can accept; the EULA only takes effect if a Pro license is later activated).
  2. Prompt for mail-server hostname (FQDN), console address, host IP, upstream DNS forwarders, and the four storage mount paths (Data, Archive, Vmail, Nextcloud).
  3. Generate all secrets and per-service config files (LDAP secrets, DB passwords, Authelia session keys, OIDC keypair, self-signed bootstrap cert, etc.).
  4. Render docker-compose.override.yml to bind the tier paths into the right containers.
  5. Run docker compose up -d --build to pull images and start the stack.
  6. Initialize all databases (Hermes, Authelia, Nextcloud, OpenDMARC, CipherMail, Syslog), populate the LDAP base structure, create the initial admin user, and pre-provision the Nextcloud admin.
  7. Print an installation summary with the admin console URL and one-time admin credentials.

3. Access the consoles

After install completes:

  • Admin Console: https://<console-host>/admin/
  • User Portal: https://<console-host>/users/
  • Nextcloud: https://<console-host>/nc/

The installer prints the initial admin username and password on completion. Change the password on first login.

Updating

Hermes SEG ships a single-command update orchestrator that handles git pull, image refresh, schema migrations, service restarts, and post-upgrade hooks in one go.

Update to the latest release

cd /opt/hermes-seg-docker-gl
sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh

This polls GitHub for the latest release, fetches the new tag, pulls updated images, applies any per-release schema/CFML/script artifacts, restarts services that need to be restarted, and runs the post-upgrade hook.

Update to a specific release

sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh v260601

Preview without applying changes

sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh --dry-run

Other flags

Flag Purpose
--skip-git Don't pull new code (containers + artifacts only)
--skip-compose Don't touch docker images (git + artifacts only)
--yes Skip the interactive confirmation prompt
--help Show full usage

For the full release-and-update methodology — including how artifacts are organized, idempotency rules, and the orchestrator's five-phase pipeline — see docs/install/release-and-update-methodology.md.

Configuration

After the installer completes, the admin will be guided through first-run configuration tasks in the admin console:

  1. Confirm console host — the install captures this at prompt time; admins can change it later under System > Console Settings.
  2. Configure DNS records — install summary prints the recommended SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and MX records to add. Use a real DNS-resolvable hostname before requesting any production Let's Encrypt certificate.
  3. Add domains — under Email Server > Domains (mailbox hosting) or Email Relay > Domains (gateway / relay mode).
  4. Create mailboxes or relay recipients — under Email Server > Mailboxes or Email Relay > Recipients.
  5. Set up SSL — either upload a 3rd-party certificate (System > System Certificates) or request a free Let's Encrypt certificate via the same page.

Detailed configuration walkthroughs — including SPF/DKIM/DMARC setup, mailbox provisioning, relay vs. mail-server modes, and the encryption gateway — live in the Documentation section.

Recovery and maintenance tools

The install script also provides a set of recovery and maintenance flags:

Command Purpose
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --show-config Display current storage paths and configuration
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --show-summary Reprint the post-install summary (admin URL, credentials reminder)
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --apply-schema Lower-level schema-only update (the canonical update path is system_update_docker.sh)
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --init-db Re-run phase 2 (post-container) initialization only — recovery flag for partial installs
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --generate-secrets Regenerate per-service secrets and re-render derived configs
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --configure-storage Re-prompt for storage mount paths and regenerate the override file
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --wipe Destructive. Tear down everything (containers, volumes, credentials, install state) for a fresh start. Requires double confirmation.

Run any of the above with --help for full usage. The installer is idempotent: re-running it (or any of its sub-steps) on an already-installed system skips already-completed work via state guards.

Documentation

In-repo documentation

  • docs/install/release-and-update-methodology.md — canonical reference for how Hermes is released, distributed, and upgraded
  • docs/install/storage-topology.md — the five-tier storage model (Config / Data / Archive / Vmail / Nextcloud)

Per-release change log

Each release's change log lives on its GitHub Release page — one body per tag, scoped to that release only.

Online documentation

Operator and end-user documentation is published at docs.deeztek.com/shelves/hermes-seg-docker, organized into:

  • Installation & Reference — Get Started, Release & Update Methodology, Storage Topology, Email Flow
  • Administrator Guide — full reference for system administrators (60 pages across 7 chapters)
  • User Guide — end-user portal documentation (11 pages)

Support

Channel Use it for
GitHub Discussions Long-form Q&A, "how do I…", configuration help. Searchable.
Matrix #hermesseg:matrix.org Real-time community chat.
Telegram HermesSEG Same audience as Matrix, different client.
GitHub Issues Bugs and feature requests.
helpdesk.deeztek.com Paid support tickets (Pro license holders).
hermesseg.io/support All support options in one place + Support Terms & Conditions.

Stay updated: subscribe to release notes and security advisories at hermesseg.io (newsletter signup in the footer).

License

Hermes Secure Email Gateway Community Edition is free software licensed under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0.

Hermes Secure Email Gateway Pro Edition is not free software. It is covered by the Hermes Secure Email Gateway Pro End-User License Agreement.

For a small single-disk test install where all tiers collapse onto one disk, ~275 GB total (thin-provisioned) is a comfortable starting point. Network Static IP or DHCP reservation. See docs for the full inbound + outbound port list (anti-spam services need TCP/2703, UDP/6277, TCP/24441, etc.) Optional but recommended For a production install, give each storage tier its own physical or virtual disk — separate from the OS disk and from each other. The tiers have deliberately different I/O and growth profiles, so isolating them lets you match disk to workload and size/expand each independently: Tier Disk it wants Why a dedicated disk Data Fast SSD High write rate (databases, logs, Postfix queue) and backup-critical — isolating it from the OS disk is the single biggest performance win Archive Commodity bulk Quarantine archive grows unboundedly with retention; keep that growth off the DB/OS disk Vmail Commodity bulk Dovecot mailboxes scale with users × quota; size and grow independently Nextcloud Commodity bulk User files + Redis cache; size for file-storage growth independently None of this is strictly required — small or test deployments can collapse the tiers onto one disk (point Archive, Vmail, and Nextcloud at the same path as Data; see Storage topology above). But for any install carrying real mail volume, a dedicated disk per tier is the recommended layout: it keeps the latency-sensitive Data tier fast, and prevents unbounded quarantine / mailbox / file growth from ever filling the database disk. If you don't want a secondary drive for a given tier, simply create the directory on your primary disk (e.g., mkdir /mnt/data /mnt/archive /mnt/vmail /mnt/files) and point the installer at it. Installation 1. Clone the repository Clone the repository wherever you'd like Hermes installed: sudo git clone https://github.com/deeztek/Hermes-Secure-Email-Gateway.git cd Hermes-Secure-Email-Gateway 2. Run the installer sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh The installer runs the full install in a single session and takes 10–30 minutes on a fresh host (mostly image downloads and fail2ban container build). The installer will: Display the Pro EULA and ask for acceptance (Community Edition users can accept; the EULA only takes effect if a Pro license is later activated). Prompt for mail-server hostname (FQDN), console address, host IP, upstream DNS forwarders, and the four storage mount paths (Data, Archive, Vmail, Nextcloud). Generate all secrets and per-service config files (LDAP secrets, DB passwords, Authelia session keys, OIDC keypair, self-signed bootstrap cert, etc.). Render docker-compose.override.yml to bind the tier paths into the right containers. Run docker compose up -d --build to pull images and start the stack. Initialize all databases (Hermes, Authelia, Nextcloud, OpenDMARC, CipherMail, Syslog), populate the LDAP base structure, create the initial admin user, and pre-provision the Nextcloud admin. Print an installation summary with the admin console URL and one-time admin credentials. 3. Access the consoles After install completes: Admin Console: https://<console-host>/admin/ User Portal: https://<console-host>/users/ Nextcloud: https://<console-host>/nc/ The installer prints the initial admin username and password on completion. Change the password on first login. Updating Hermes SEG ships a single-command update orchestrator that handles git pull, image refresh, schema migrations, service restarts, and post-upgrade hooks in one go. Update to the latest release cd /opt/hermes-seg-docker-gl sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh This polls GitHub for the latest release, fetches the new tag, pulls updated images, applies any per-release schema/CFML/script artifacts, restarts services that need to be restarted, and runs the post-upgrade hook. Update to a specific release sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh v260601 Preview without applying changes sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh --dry-run Other flags Flag Purpose --skip-git Don't pull new code (containers + artifacts only) --skip-compose Don't touch docker images (git + artifacts only) --yes Skip the interactive confirmation prompt --help Show full usage For the full release-and-update methodology — including how artifacts are organized, idempotency rules, and the orchestrator's five-phase pipeline — see docs/install/release-and-update-methodology.md. Configuration After the installer completes, the admin will be guided through first-run configuration tasks in the admin console: Confirm console host — the install captures this at prompt time; admins can change it later under System > Console Settings. Configure DNS records — install summary prints the recommended SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and MX records to add. Use a real DNS-resolvable hostname before requesting any production Let's Encrypt certificate. Add domains — under Email Server > Domains (mailbox hosting) or Email Relay > Domains (gateway / relay mode). Create mailboxes or relay recipients — under Email Server > Mailboxes or Email Relay > Recipients. Set up SSL — either upload a 3rd-party certificate (System > System Certificates) or request a free Let's Encrypt certificate via the same page. Detailed configuration walkthroughs — including SPF/DKIM/DMARC setup, mailbox provisioning, relay vs. mail-server modes, and the encryption gateway — live in the Documentation section. Recovery and maintenance tools The install script also provides a set of recovery and maintenance flags: Command Purpose sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --show-config Display current storage paths and configuration sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --show-summary Reprint the post-install summary (admin URL, credentials reminder) sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --apply-schema Lower-level schema-only update (the canonical update path is system_update_docker.sh) sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --init-db Re-run phase 2 (post-container) initialization only — recovery flag for partial installs sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --generate-secrets Regenerate per-service secrets and re-render derived configs sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --configure-storage Re-prompt for storage mount paths and regenerate the override file sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --wipe Destructive. Tear down everything (containers, volumes, credentials, install state) for a fresh start. Requires double confirmation. Run any of the above with --help for full usage. The installer is idempotent: re-running it (or any of its sub-steps) on an already-installed system skips already-completed work via state guards. Documentation In-repo documentation docs/install/release-and-update-methodology.md — canonical reference for how Hermes is released, distributed, and upgraded docs/install/storage-topology.md — the five-tier storage model (Config / Data / Archive / Vmail / Nextcloud) Per-release change log Each release's change log lives on its GitHub Release page — one body per tag, scoped to that release only. Online documentation Operator and end-user documentation is published at docs.deeztek.com/shelves/hermes-seg-docker, organized into: Installation & Reference — Get Started, Release & Update Methodology, Storage Topology, Email Flow Administrator Guide — full reference for system administrators (60 pages across 7 chapters) User Guide — end-user portal documentation (11 pages) Support Channel Use it for GitHub Discussions Long-form Q&A, "how do I…", configuration help. Searchable. Matrix #hermesseg:matrix.org Real-time community chat. Telegram HermesSEG Same audience as Matrix, different client. GitHub Issues Bugs and feature requests. helpdesk.deeztek.com Paid support tickets (Pro license holders). hermesseg.io/support All support options in one place + Support Terms & Conditions. Stay updated: subscribe to release notes and security advisories at hermesseg.io (newsletter signup in the footer). blocks.html_en: Hermes Secure Email Gateway is a Free Open Source Secure Email Gateway and Email Server.

sentences.en: Hermes Secure Email Gateway is a Free Open Source Secure Email Gateway and Email Server.
sentences.zh: Hermes 安全电子邮件网关是一个免费的开源安全电子邮件网关和电子邮件服务器。

blocks.html_en: It provides spam, virus, and malware protection through Apache SpamAssassin, ClamAV, and Amavisd-new; full in-transit and at-rest email encryption via SMTP TLS, S/MIME, PGP, encrypted PDF (powered by CipherMail), and Dovecot mail-crypt; email archiving; integrated mailbox hosting on Dovecot with per-user quotas, aliases, shared folders, Sieve rules, vacation auto-reply, and mobile-device autoconfiguration; file sync, webmail, calendars (CalDAV), and contacts (CardDAV) through Nextcloud; a local user directory and single sign-on via OpenLDAP and Authelia — with multi-factor authentication via TOTP, WebAuthn, and Duo Push; and modern email authentication standards including SPF, DKIM signing and verification, DMARC, and ARC through OpenDKIM, OpenDMARC, and OpenARC.

blocks.html_en: Time-of-click URL protection for inbound mail — comparable to Microsoft Safe Links and Proofpoint URL Defense. Links are rewritten to a Hermes redirect and the destination's reputation is checked at the moment the user clicks — catching links weaponized after delivery. Layered verdicts (heuristics + URLhaus / OpenPhish blocklist feeds + optional Google Safe Browsing / VirusTotal), open-redirect detection, an operator-managed list of abused cloud-storage / redirector hosts, and optional guarded redirect-chain following. Admin-configurable per-tier actions and outbound link restoration. Runs in-stack or on a separate host.

content_html_zh:
自托管电子邮件安全、邮件服务器和 Nextcloud — 一个堆栈，一个 Docker Compose 文件。 开源垃圾邮件和恶意软件过滤、S/MIME 和 PGP 加密、DKIM/DMARC/ARC、邮箱、网络邮件和 SSO。 Pro 添加了点击时间链接保护 - 您需要为 Proofpoint 或 Mimecast 付费的入站链接防御。

功能 · 专业版 · 定价 · 文档 · 支持

可部署为 Microsoft 365/Google Workspace/Exchange 前端的网关、具有内置邮箱的完整邮件服务器或混合模式。

About

Hermes 安全电子邮件网关是一个免费的开源安全电子邮件网关和电子邮件服务器。

它通过 Apache SpamAssassin、ClamAV 和 Amavisd-new 提供垃圾邮件、病毒和恶意软件防护；通过 SMTP TLS、S/MIME、PGP、加密 PDF（由 CipherMail 提供支持）和 Dovecot mail-crypt 实现完整的传输中和静态电子邮件加密；电子邮件归档； Dovecot 上的集成邮箱托管与每用户配额、别名、共享文件夹、Sieve 规则、假期自动回复和移动设备自动配置；通过 Nextcloud 进行文件同步、网络邮件、日历 (CalDAV) 和联系人 (CardDAV)；本地用户目录以及通过 OpenLDAP 和 Authelia 的单点登录 — 通过 TOTP、WebAuthn 和 Duo Push 进行多重身份验证；现代电子邮件身份验证标准，包括 SPF、DKIM 签名和验证、DMARC 以及通过 OpenDKIM、OpenDMARC 和 OpenARC 进行的 ARC。

Hermes 将这些开源技术结合在一个基于 Web 的统一管理控制台下，以便轻松管理组织的入站和出站电子邮件、邮箱用户、加密密钥和身份验证策略。 最终用户可以获得一个自助服务门户，用于管理自己的签名、筛选规则、假期消息、应用程序密码和移动设备配置文件。

Hermes 可以通过三种方式部署：

  • 作为现有邮件解决方案（内部 Exchange/Postfix、Google Workspace、Microsoft 365）前面的网关。 Hermes 扫描、过滤、加密邮件并将其转发到您的后端。
  • 作为一个完整的邮件服务器，具有内置邮箱托管、网络邮件、文件同步、日历和联系人。 不需要外部邮件后端。
  • 作为混合 — 某些域的网关和同一安装中其他域的邮件服务器。 中继（网关）域和邮箱（邮件服务器）域共存于单个 Hermes 部署中。

该 Docker 版本将整个堆栈打包为一组由 Docker Compose 管理的容器，用可移植、可重复的部署取代了传统的裸机 Ubuntu 安装程序。

正在寻找托管版本？ Hermes 在设计上是自托管的。 如果您想支持开发并获得供应商支持，请参阅专业定价 — 按服务器许可，无按邮箱费用。

版本

Hermes 有两个版本：

版 执照 你得到什么
社区 AGPL v3 — 免费、开源 整个邮件网关和电子邮件服务器堆栈。 所有核心安全、加密、邮箱托管和管理功能。
Pro 商业 — 请参阅 EULA 社区中的一切以及 6 项高级功能（请参阅下面的专业功能）。 定价 →

Pro 许可证需单独购买。 社区版不需要许可证文件，无需许可证文件即可完全运行。

该项目已获得 AGPLv3 许可（LICENSE）；一小组专有专业版文件不包含在 AGPL 授权范围内，并受 Pro EULA 管辖。 请参阅 LICENSING.md 了解权威细分 - 每个文件标头控制适用的许可证。

特征

一份精简清单。 请参阅 hermesseg.io/features 了解带有屏幕截图的完整功能页面。

邮件安全（社区）

  • 垃圾邮件防护（Apache SpamAssassin、postscreen、RBL 配置、发件人/收件人/网络阻止允许列表、全局发件人过滤器）
  • 防病毒保护（ClamAV 通过 Amavisd-new，内置提要：ClamAV 官方 + URLhaus）
  • 恶意软件源管理（通过 Fangfrisch 管理）— 配置其他第 3 方签名源，包括 SaneSecurity、MalwarePatrol、SecuriteInfo、TwinWave、ClamPunch、RFXN、InterServer、Ditekshen 等
  • 每个收件人的垃圾邮件/病毒/文件策略
  • 自定义消息规则、分数覆盖、自定义文件表达式/扩展名/规则
  • 隔离、消息历史记录搜索、队列管理、训练为垃圾邮件/火腿、发布给收件人、下载消息

加密和身份验证（社区）

  • 传输中电子邮件加密：SMTP TLS、S/MIME、PGP（通过 CipherMail）
  • 静态加密：Dovecot 邮件加密货币
  • 为没有 S/MIME 或 PGP 的收件人提供加密的 PDF 电子邮件
  • 多重身份验证 (Authelia)：TOTP、WebAuthn、Duo Push
  • 用于管理员、邮箱用户和中继用户的本地 LDAP 用户存储（内置 OpenLDAP）
  • SMTP / IMAP / DAV 客户端的应用程序密码（与主帐户密码分开）
  • 免费的 Let's Encrypt (ACME) TLS 证书 — 管理控制台和每个邮箱域 (SAN) 自动颁发和自动续订，以及对第 3 方证书的导入/CSR 支持
  • haveibeenpwned 密码检查集成

电子邮件标准（社区）

  • SPF检查、DKIM检查+签名、DMARC验证（OpenDMARC）
  • ARC（经过验证的接收链）签名和验证 (OpenARC) — 跨受信任的转发器保留身份验证结果
  • DKIM 密钥生成和管理 (UI)
  • 用于区分出站签名与入站验证行为的多实例 OpenDKIM
  • DMARC 报告汇总和报告

电子邮件服务器/邮箱托管（社区）

  • 本地邮箱托管 (Dovecot 2.4)，支持 IMAPS / POP3S / 提交 (587/465) / LMTP
  • 每个域和每个邮箱的配额
  • 邮箱别名和转发器
  • 共享邮箱和共享文件夹
  • 用户定义的筛选规则
  • 带有日期范围和每个地址过滤的假期自动回复
  • 通过签名的 .mobileconfig 配置文件 (iOS) 和 CalDAV/CardDAV 自动发现进行移动设备自动配置
  • 个人电子邮件签名（丰富的 HTML，带有模板库）
  • 外部发件人横幅（来自组织外部的入站邮件会获得可视横幅）

Nextcloud 集成（社区）

  • 文件同步（Nextcloud 文件）
  • 网络邮件（Nextcloud 邮件）
  • 日历 (CalDAV) 和联系人 (CardDAV)
  • 通过 Authelia OIDC 单点登录
  • 首次登录时预先配置 Nextcloud 用户帐户

管理和用户体验（社区）

  • 现代 AdminLTE 4 / Bootstrap 5 管理员控制台
  • 用户自助服务门户（每个邮箱）
  • 具有系统资源监控的实时仪表板
  • 带有可视化图表的消息统计
  • 计划任务 UI（数据库支持的 Ofelia 作业管理）
  • 可搜索的系统事件日志
  • 内部 CA 管理 (S/MIME)
  • DNS 解析器（未绑定、本地递归）
  • 控制台主机和域管理

专业版功能

专业版在社区的所有功能之上添加了以下功能。 完整专业版功能页面 →

专业功能 它的作用
电子邮件免责声明 每个域的出站免责声明模板，在 milter 级别应用。 具有可重用模板的基于表单的模板渲染器。
组织签名 集中管理的每个域员工签名模板，具有占位符替换功能（员工姓名、职务、电话、电子邮件、部门、组织信息）。 在每条出站消息上呈现。 社区版仅具有个人签名（每用户，自由格式）。
入侵防御 (IPS) 用于管理 Fail2ban 监狱、禁令阈值、禁令持续时间、白名单的 Web UI。 实时查看活动禁令。
控制台防火墙 用于管理保护管理控制台的主机防火墙（端口白名单、源 IP 限制）的 Web UI。
LDAP 远程验证 对一台或多台外部 LDAP 服务器（包括 Microsoft Active Directory）进行逐域直通身份验证。 最终用户根据您现有的目录进行身份验证； Hermes 在首次成功登录时提供邮箱。 支持 STARTTLS 和 LDAPS。
链接卫士 针对入站邮件的点击时 URL 保护 — 与 Microsoft Safe Links 和 Proofpoint URL Defense 相当。 链接被重写为 Hermes 重定向，并在用户点击时检查目的地的声誉 - 捕获交付后武器化的链接。 分层判决（启发式 + URLhaus / OpenPhish 黑名单提要 + 可选的 Google 安全浏览 / VirusTotal）、开放重定向检测、运营商管理的滥用云存储/重定向器主机列表以及可选的受保护重定向链跟踪。 管理员可配置的每层操作和出站链接恢复。 在堆栈内或单独的主机上运行。

建筑学

Hermes SEG Docker 版本作为由 Docker Compose 编排的 19 个容器运行：

容器 目的
爱马仕_unbound 堆栈的递归 DNS 解析器
Hermes数据库服务器 MariaDB — Hermes、Authelia、Nextcloud、OpenDMARC、CipherMail、Syslog 数据库
爱马仕_奥菲莉亚 计划任务运行程序（cron 替代）
Hermes_nginx 反向代理 + SSL 终止（管理控制台、用户门户、Nextcloud、CipherMail UI）
爱马仕_authelia 具有 MFA 的 SSO 门户（TOTP / WebAuthn / Duo Push）
Hermes_authelia_redis Authelia 的会话存储
Hermes_commandbox CFML 应用程序服务器 (Lucee) — 托管管理控制台 + 用户门户
Hermes_postfix_dkim Postfix MTA + OpenDKIM 签名者/验证者
爱马仕_dmarc OpenDMARC 验证器 + 报告聚合器
爱马仕_openarc OpenARC 链签名者/验证者
Hermes_mail_filter Amavisd-new + SpamAssassin + ClamAV 内容过滤器
爱马仕身体米尔特 正文修改米尔特 — 签名、免责声明、外部发件人横幅和 Link Guard 链接重写/恢复
Hermes_linkguard Link Guard 点击时间安全链接端点（URL 重写 + 点击时间信誉）
Hermes_ciphermail S/MIME、PGP、加密-PDF加密网关
Hermes_fail2ban 暴力预防（Dovecot、Authelia 监狱）
爱马仕_鸽子 IMAP / POP3 / 提交 / LMTP / Sieve 服务器
爱马仕LDAP OpenLDAP — 本地用户目录（管理员、邮箱、中继用户）
爱马仕_nextcloud 文件同步、网络邮件、CalDAV、CardDAV
Hermes_nextcloud_redis Nextcloud 的缓存 + 锁定后端

存储拓扑

Hermes 将存储划分为五个独立层，以便每个层都可以位于适合其工作负载的正确类型的磁盘上：

Tier 默认路径 内容 存储配置文件
配置 安装root（隐式） 回购工作树，生成的配置，秘密，.env 快速固态硬盘；按回购位置调整大小
Data /mnt/数据 数据库、服务日志、邮件过滤器状态、Postfix 队列 快速固态硬盘；根据数据库增长和日志保留调整大小。 高写入速率，备份关键。
档案 /mnt/存档 阿马维斯检疫档案 廉价散装；大小适合保留策略×隔离流入。 无边生长，冷通。
Vmail /mnt/vmail 鸽舍邮箱 廉价散装；用户大小 × 配额
下一云 /mnt/文件 Nextcloud应用程序+用户文件+Redis缓存 廉价散装；适合用户文件存储的大小

较小的部署可以折叠层 - 将 Archive、Vmail 和 Nextcloud 指向与单磁盘安装的数据相同的路径。 安装程序会提示输入每个路径；所有四个操作员选择的安装点都是强制性的（空值在组合替换期间存在相对路径解析的风险）。

请参阅 docs.deeztek.com · 存储拓扑（5 层）或存储库中的 docs/install/storage-topology.md 上的规范参考。

要求

Item 推荐
主机操作系统 任何能够运行 Docker Engine 24.0+ 和 Compose v2 的 Linux 发行版。 测试参考：Ubuntu 24.04 Server。
码头工人 引擎24.0+
Docker 组合 v2
CPU 最少 4 个 vCPU，更多可满足更高的邮件量
RAM 最低 8 GB，建议生产使用 16 GB 以上
Disk 配置/操作系统磁盘：至少 120 GB — 操作系统、Docker 引擎、完整的 Hermes 映像集 + 正在运行的容器、存储库和安装/服务日志。 四个数据层（数据、存档、Vmail、Nextcloud）的大小根据您的预期使用情况进行调整 - 请参阅存储拓扑。 对于所有层都折叠到一个磁盘上的小型单磁盘测试安装，总计约 275 GB（精简配置）是一个合适的起点。
网络 静态 IP 或 DHCP 保留。 请参阅文档了解完整的入站 + 出站端口列表（反垃圾邮件服务需要 TCP/2703、UDP/6277、TCP/24441 等）

可选但推荐

对于生产安装，为每个存储层提供自己的物理或虚拟磁盘 - 与操作系统磁盘以及彼此之间分开。 这些层故意具有不同的 I/O 和增长配置文件，因此隔离它们可以让您将磁盘与工作负载相匹配，并独立地调整/扩展每个层：

Tier 想要的磁盘 为什么要使用专用磁盘
Data 快速固态硬盘 高写入速率（数据库、日志、Postfix 队列）和备份关键 — 将其与操作系统磁盘隔离是最大的性能优势
档案 商品散装 隔离档案随着保留量的增加而无限增长；让 DB/OS 磁盘不再增长
Vmail 商品散装 Dovecot邮箱随用户×配额扩展；大小和独立生长
下一云 商品散装 用户文件+Redis缓存；文件存储大小独立增长

这些都不是严格要求的 - 小型或测试部署可以将各层折叠到一个磁盘上（将 Archive、Vmail 和 Nextcloud 指向与数据相同的路径；请参阅上面的存储拓扑）。 但对于任何承载实际邮件量的安装，建议每层使用专用磁盘：它使延迟敏感的数据层保持快速，并防止无限制的隔离/邮箱/文件增长填满数据库磁盘。

如果您不需要给定层的辅助驱动器，只需在主磁盘上创建目录（例如 mkdir /mnt/data /mnt/archive /mnt/vmail /mnt/files）并将安装程序指向该目录。

安装

1. 克隆存储库

将存储库克隆到您想要安装 Hermes 的位置：

sudo git clone https://github.com/deeztek/Hermes-Secure-Email-Gateway.git
cd Hermes-Secure-Email-Gateway

2. 运行安装程序

sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh

安装程序在单个会话中运行完整安装，并在新主机上花费 10-30 分钟（主要是图像下载和fail2ban 容器构建）。

安装程序将：

  1. 显示 Pro EULA 并请求接受（社区版用户可以接受；EULA 仅在稍后激活 Pro 许可证后才生效）。
  2. 提示输入邮件服务器主机名 (FQDN)、控制台地址、主机 IP、上游 DNS 转发器和四个存储安装路径（Data、Archive、Vmail、Nextcloud）。
  3. 生成所有机密和每个服务的配置文件（LDAP 机密、数据库密码、Authelia 会话密钥、OIDC 密钥对、自签名引导证书等）。
  4. 渲染 docker-compose.override.yml 以将层路径绑定到正确的容器中。
  5. 运行 docker compose up -d --build 来拉取镜像并启动堆栈。
  6. 初始化所有数据库（Hermes、Authelia、Nextcloud、OpenDMARC、CipherMail、Syslog），填充 LDAP 基本结构，创建初始管理员用户，并预配置 Nextcloud 管理员。
  7. 使用管理控制台 URL 和一次性管理凭据打印安装摘要。

3. 访问控制台

安装完成后：

  • 管理控制台：https://<console-host>/admin/
  • 用户门户：https://<console-host>/users/
  • Nextcloud：https://<控制台主机>/nc/

安装程序完成后会打印初始管理员用户名和密码。 首次登录时更改密码。

更新中

Hermes SEG 提供了一个单命令更新协调器，可以一次性处理 git pull、图像刷新、架构迁移、服务重新启动和升级后挂钩。

更新到最新版本

cd /opt/hermes-seg-docker-gl
sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh

这将轮询 GitHub 以获取最新版本，获取新标签，提取更新的图像，应用任何每个版本的架构/CFML/脚本工件，重新启动需要重新启动的服务，并运行升级后挂钩。

更新到特定版本

sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh v260601

预览而不应用更改

sudo ./scripts/system_update_docker.sh --dry-run

其他旗帜

Flag 目的
--跳过 git 不要提取新代码（仅限容器+工件）
--跳过撰写 不要触摸 docker 镜像（仅限 git + 工件）
--yes 跳过交互式确认提示
--help 显示完整使用情况

有关完整的发布和更新方法（包括工件的组织方式、幂等性规则和编排器的五阶段管道），请参阅 docs/install/release-and-update-methodology.md。

配置

安装程序完成后，将指导管理员在管理控制台中完成首次运行的配置任务：

  1. 确认控制台主机 — 安装程序会在提示时捕获此信息；管理员可以稍后在“系统”>“控制台设置”下更改它。
  2. 配置 DNS 记录 — 安装摘要打印建议添加的 SPF、DKIM、DMARC 和 MX 记录。 在请求任何生产 Let's Encrypt 证书之前，请使用真实的 DNS 可解析主机名。
  3. 添加域 - 在电子邮件服务器 > 域（邮箱托管）或电子邮件中继 > 域（网关/中继模式）下。
  4. 创建邮箱或中继收件人 - 在电子邮件服务器 > 邮箱或电子邮件中继 > 收件人下。
  5. 设置 SSL — 上传第 3 方证书（系统 > 系统证书）或通过同一页面请求免费的 Let's Encrypt 证书。

详细的配置演练 — 包括 SPF/DKIM/DMARC 设置、邮箱配置、中继与邮件服务器模式以及加密网关 — 位于文档部分。

恢复和维护工具

安装脚本还提供了一组恢复和维护标志：

命令 目的
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --show-config 显示当前存储路径和配置
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --show-summary 重新打印安装后摘要（管理 URL、凭据提醒）
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --apply-schema 仅较低级别的架构更新（规范更新路径是system_update_docker.sh）
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --init-db 仅重新运行第 2 阶段（容器后）初始化 — 部分安装的恢复标志
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --generate-secrets 重新生成每个服务的机密并重新渲染派生配置
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --configure-storage 重新提示存储挂载路径并重新生成覆盖文件
sudo ./scripts/install_hermes_docker.sh --wipe 具有破坏性。 拆除所有内容（容器、卷、凭据、安装状态）以重新开始。 需要双重确认。

使用 --help 运行以上任何命令以充分利用。 安装程序是幂等的：在已安装的系统上重新运行它（或其任何子步骤）会通过状态防护跳过已完成的工作。
